rulings.Case Title: GREGORY ESPITALLIER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Compliance With Subpoenas And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, November 12, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Volkswagen Group of America, Inc.¿s motion to enforce compliance with subpoenas is granted in part. Plaintiff must provide defendant with the witnesses¿ last known addresses and phone numbers within five days. ¿C/o Brayton Purcell¿ is an insufficient response for contact information. By making such a response and not providing any further contact information, plaintiff created the situation that resulted in the events described in the moving and reply papers. Defendant¿s request for sanctions is granted in the amount of $2500. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ALBERT LAWRENCE LEVENE VS. BONDEX INTERNATIONAL, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion Of Defendant Mccord Corporation To Compel Further Responses To Its Special Interrogatories, Request For Production Of Documents, Request For Admissions And Form Interrogatories And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, November 12, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Defendant McCord Corporation¿s motion to compel further discovery responses was previously continued at the parties¿ request. The court assumes that the parties continued the hearing to engage in meet and confer efforts. If any issues remain unresolved, by 4:30 p.m. today the parties must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org stating, without argument, what issues remain, and then appear at the hearing. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DONAT LENNEY et al VS. ARMSTRONG INTERNATIONAL, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Have Requests For Admissions Deemed Admitted
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, November 12, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Minton¿s Lumber and Supply Company¿s motion to have requests for admissions deemed admitted will be heard by the trial judge = (220/HEK)
